Pipeline Conditions & Balancing Guidelines
(1) Line pack is normal.
(2) All shippers and delivery point operators should align daily scheduled nominations and physical receipts and deliveries.
(3) Requested on- and off-system balancing is subject to approval by Kern River.

Last updated: 7/11/2026 7:22:39 AM.
Planned Service Outage
Maintenance Schedule
System Line Pack
Effective Feb. 24, 2025, the High/Low Line Pack range is 350,000 Mcsf. The high/low line pack band is adjusted from time-to-time for reasons including, but not limited to, seasonal weather changes, impacts due to maintenance events, and changes to shipper nomination patterns.

Disclaimer
The information posted to this web site is provided solely for informational purposes. Although Kern River has used reasonable efforts to assure its accuracy and timeliness, this information can be negatively impacted by delays or errors in electronic transfers, data entry, communication failures, or other causes. No representation is made that the contents are free from error. Kern River assumes no responsibility for use of, or reliance on, this information by any party, and specifically advises such parties to discuss any decisions or actions related hereto with their own advisors and experts.


Operationally Available Capacity

Kern River considers line pack and operating conditions in all segments of the system, including Kern River's wholly-owned mainline and the Kern River/Mojave Common Facilities, when operating capacity at mainline compressors is determined.
